question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a network value containing the NETWORK_PREFIX_DELIMITER '-' (e.g., 'mainnet-evil') so that getNetwork() strips the suffix and returns 'mainnet', causing the importer to silently resolve the wrong canonical network name and ingest stream files from an attacker-influenced bucket, leading to transaction history reorganization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetworkPrefix()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a network string like 'demo-attacker' so that getNetworkPrefix() returns 'attacker', causing downstream path-resolution logic in S3StreamFileProvider to construct a bucket prefix pointing to attacker-controlled storage, resulting in fraudulent stream files being ingested and transaction history being reordered?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFileData whose filename is shorter than 10 characters, causing getArchiveDestinationFolderPath() to throw a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException or silently produce a malformed path, disrupting the archive of verified stream files and allowing re-ingestion of previously rejected files to reorganize transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a StreamFileData filename whose first 10 characters contain path-traversal sequences (e.g., '../../../../') so that getArchiveDestinationFolderPath() resolves to an unintended directory, overwriting legitimate archived stream files with attacker-controlled content and enabling transaction history manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where groupByDay is false (default is true but can be toggled), causing all stream files to be archived into the same flat streamPath directory, making it impossible to distinguish files by date and allowing an attacker to substitute older stream files for newer ones to reorder transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: HederaNetwork.isAllowAnonymousAccess()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that isAllowAnonymousAccess() returns true only for 'demo' network, and by influencing the network property to be set to 'demo', cause the importer to use anonymous (unauthenticated) cloud storage access, allowing the attacker to serve malicious stream files from a public bucket with the same name and reorganize transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: HederaNetwork.hasCutover()] Can an unprivileged external user influence the network property to a value other than 'mainnet' or 'testnet' so that hasCutover() returns false, disabling the record-to-block-stream cutover logic and causing the importer to continue ingesting from the old record stream bucket even after the network has switched, resulting in a stale or reorganized view of transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: HederaNetwork.getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a network name that does not match any key in NETWORK_DEFAULT_BUCKETS (e.g., 'other'), causing getBucketName() to return an empty string, which then forces the importer to fall back to a user-configurable bucketName that could point to attacker-controlled storage, enabling transaction history reorganization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: HederaNetwork.getBlockStreamBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a network name not present in NETWORK_DEFAULT_BUCKETS so that getBlockStreamBucketName() returns an empty string, causing BlockProperties.getBucketName() to fall back to a blank or operator-misconfigured bucket name, allowing attacker-controlled block stream files to be ingested and transaction history to be reorganized?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a network value with only a delimiter and no prefix (e.g., '-suffix'), causing StringUtils.substringBefore to return an empty string, which then fails the @NotBlank validation or causes downstream logic to resolve an empty network name to an unintended bucket, leading to ingestion of attacker-controlled stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ConsensusMode enum] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EQUAL ConsensusMode (all nodes equally weighted) to reduce the effective consensus threshold, making it easier for a minority of colluding nodes to achieve consensus on a fraudulent stream file, thereby reorganizing transaction history without needing a stake majority?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ConsensusMode enum] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the STAKE ConsensusMode (uses all nodes including those not in the address book) to include rogue nodes with artificially high stake values in the consensus calculation, diluting the weight of legitimate nodes and allowing fraudulent stream files to reach consensus, reorganizing transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: ConsensusMode enum] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the difference between STAKE and STAKE_IN_ADDRESS_BOOK modes: when STAKE is used, nodes outside the address book contribute to totalStake, potentially allowing an attacker who can inject node stake records to inflate totalStake and lower the effective consensus ratio, enabling fraudulent stream file acceptance?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: startDate field] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a condition where startDate is set to a past timestamp, causing DateRangeCalculator to re-download and re-parse stream files from before the last known good file, effectively replaying and potentially substituting historical transactions to reorganize the transaction history stored in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: endDate field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the endDate field being set to a past value, causing the importer to stop ingesting new stream files prematurely, creating a gap in the transaction history that could be filled with attacker-controlled data upon restart, effectively reorganizing the visible transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: startBlockNumber field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the startBlockNumber field being set to -1 (the minimum allowed by @Min(-1)) to instruct the block node subscriber to start from the earliest available block, causing the importer to re-ingest all historical blocks and potentially overwrite existing transaction records with attacker-modified block content?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: startBlockNumber field] Can an unprivileged external user set startBlockNumber to a specific past block number, causing the importer to skip all blocks between the last ingested block and the specified start, creating gaps in the transaction history that misrepresent the true sequence of on-chain events?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: endBlockNumber field] Can an unprivileged external user set endBlockNumber to a value less than the current chain tip, causing the importer to stop processing blocks before the latest finalized block, truncating the visible transaction history and creating a false impression of the network state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: endBlockNumber field] Can an unprivileged external user set endBlockNumber to 0 (the minimum allowed by @PositiveOrZero), causing the importer to immediately halt block ingestion after the very first block, effectively wiping the visible transaction history beyond block 0 and reorganizing what the mirror node exposes to API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: nodePublicKey field] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted nodePublicKey value that overrides all address book node public keys in AddressBookServiceImpl.getNodes(), causing the importer to accept stream file signatures verified against the attacker's key instead of legitimate node keys, allowing fraudulent stream files to pass signature verification and reorganize transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: nodePublicKey field] Can an unprivileged external user set nodePublicKey to an empty or whitespace string, and since AddressBookServiceImpl checks StringUtils.isNotBlank(importerProperties.getNodePublicKey()) before overriding, verify whether a near-blank value (e.g., a single space) bypasses the blank check and injects a useless public key, causing all signature verifications to fail and halting ingestion of legitimate stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: initialAddressBook field] Can an unprivileged external user supply a path for initialAddressBook that points to a crafted address book file containing attacker-controlled node entries with attacker-owned public keys, causing the importer to bootstrap with a fraudulent address book and accept stream files signed by attacker-controlled nodes, reorganizing transaction history from the very start?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: initialAddressBook field] Can an unprivileged external user supply a path for initialAddressBook that points to a file with fewer nodes than the real network, reducing the totalStake denominator and lowering the consensus threshold so that fewer legitimate node signatures are required to accept a fraudulent stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp field] Can an unprivileged external user set topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp to a future timestamp, causing the SQL migration V1.23.1 to assign running_hash_version=1 to all topic messages (instead of 2), corrupting the running hash version for all historical topic messages and making it impossible to verify the integrity of the topic message sequence?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp field] Can an unprivileged external user set topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp to 0 or a very early timestamp, causing all topic messages to be assigned running_hash_version=2 regardless of their actual consensus timestamp, breaking the hash chain verification for pre-v2 topic messages and allowing history manipulation of topic message sequences?
